memoryArtifacts.headline: McGowan, Isobel  story
memoryArtifacts.description: Isobel McGowan was born in 1535 in Clackmannan, Clackmannanshire, Scotland. She married John Sharp in her hometown. They had one child during their marriage. She died in 1585 in Stirling, Stirlingshir …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Isobel McGowan於1533年出生在蘇格蘭可拉可曼南史爾可拉可曼南時，她的父親Thomas McGowan25歲，母親Marion Cunningham27歲。她於1555年與John Sharp在英國蘇格蘭可拉可曼南史爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1585年在蘇格蘭斯林史爾斯特林享年52歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isobel McGowan于1533年出生在苏格兰可拉可曼南史爾可拉可曼南时，她的父亲Thomas McGowan25岁，母亲Marion Cunningham27岁。她于1555年与John Sharp在英国苏格兰可拉可曼南史爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1585年在苏格兰斯林史爾斯特林享年52岁。

nameMeanings.name: McGowan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Siobhan, Cormac, Seamus, Aileen, Colm, Eithne, Kieran, Niall, Paddy.

Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac (a') Ghobhainn or Mac Gobhann (Scottish) or Mac (an) Ghabhann (Irish), ‘son of (the) smith’. This name has also been Anglicized as Smith and Smithson .

variants.name: McGowin
meanings: Scottish and Irish: variant of   McGowan  .

variants.name: Smithson
meanings: English (Yorkshire): from Middle English smith ‘smith’ + -son, for a son of a smith.
Irish (Dublin) and Scottish: in Scotland and Ireland, sometimes adopted for Mac Gobhann, Mac a'ghobhainn ‘son of the smith’ (see McGowan ).

History: The Smithsonian Institution in Washington, DC was founded (1846) with a bequest from the English chemist and mineralogist James Smithson (1765–1829). He was born James Lewes Macie, the illegitimate son of Hugh Smithson Percy, Duke of Northumberland. His mother, Elizabeth Macie, a widow, was a direct descendant of Henry VIII; through her family James Smithson inherited a fortune.

variants.name: Smith
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name denoting a worker in metal, especially iron, such as a blacksmith or farrier, from Middle English smith ‘smith’ (Old English smith, probably a derivative of smītan ‘to strike, hammer’). Early examples are also found in the Latin form Faber . Metal-working was one of the earliest occupations for which specialist skills were required, and its importance ensured that this term and its equivalents in other languages were the most widespread of all occupational surnames in Europe. Medieval smiths were important not only in making horseshoes, plowshares, and other domestic articles, but above all for their skill in forging swords, other weapons, and armor. This is also the most frequent of all surnames in the US. It is very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below). This surname (in any of the two possible English senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti. See also Smither .

English: from Middle English smithe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smiththe). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, occupational, for someone who worked in one, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Smitha in King's Nympton (Devon). Compare Smithey .
Irish and Scottish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac Gobhann, Irish Mac Gabhann ‘son of the smith’. See McGowan .

variants.name: Magoon
meanings: Probably an altered form of Scottish and Irish   McGowan  .Compare   Magoun  .

variants.name: Magoun
meanings: Probably an altered form of Scottish and Irish   McGowan  .Compare   Magoon  .

variants.name: McGuane
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brennan.

Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Dhubháin, a patronymic from the personal name Dubhán, a derivative of dubh ‘dark, black’. The surname has also been Anglicized (in Mayo) as McGowan . Compare Doane .

variants.name: Magowan
meanings: Irish: variant of   McGowan  .

variants.name: McGavin
meanings: Scottish and Irish: variant of   McGowan  .
Irish: variant of   McGaffin  .

variants.name: McGowen
meanings: Scottish and Irish: variant of   McGowan  .

variants.name: McGown
meanings: Scottish and Irish: variant of   McGowan  .

nameMeanings.name: Isobel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of   Isabel .

variants.name: Isabel
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

en.lifeSummary: When Isobel McGowan was born in 1533, in Clackmannan, Clackmannanshire, Scotland, her father, Thomas McGowan, was 25 and her mother, Marion Cunningham, was 27. She married John Sharp in 1555, in Clackmannanshire, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1585, in Stirling, Stirlingshire, Scotland, at the age of 52.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isobel McGowan est née en 1533, à Clackmannan, Clackmannanshire, Écosse, son père, Thomas McGowan, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Marion Cunningham, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé John Sharp en 1555, à Clackmannanshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1585, à Stirling, Stirlingshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 52 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isobel McGowan 1533 in Clackmannan, Clackmannanshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas McGowan 25 und ihre Mutter Marion Cunningham 27. Sie hat 1555 in Clackmannanshire, Schottland, United Kingdom John Sharp geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1585 im Alter von 52 Jahren in Stirling, Stirlingshire, Scotland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isobel McGowan è nata il nel 1533 nel Clackmannan, Clackmannanshire, Scotland, sua padre, Thomas McGowan, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Marion Cunningham, aveva 27 anni. Lei sposato John Sharp nel 1555, nel Clackmannanshire, Scozia, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1585, nel Stirling, Stirlingshire, Scotland, all'età di 52 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Isobel McGowanが1533年にスコットランドクラクマナンシュアイアクラクマナンで生まれた時，父Thomas McGowanは25歳，母Marion Cunninghamは27歳であった。1555年にイギリススコットランドクラクマナンシュアイアでJohn Sharpと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1585年にスコットランドスティルリングシュアイアスティルリングで52歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Isobel McGowan(이)가 1533년 스컷랜드 클랙먼아실르 클랙먼아에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas McGowan(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Marion Cunningham(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 1555년 영국 스코틀랜드 클랙먼아실르에서 John Sharp(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1585년 스컷랜드 스티를링실르 스티를링에서 52세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isobel McGowan nasceu em 1533, em Clackmannan, Clackmannanshire, Scotland, seu pai, Thomas McGowan, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Marion Cunningham, tinha 27 anos. Ela casou-se com John Sharp em 1555, em Clackmannanshire, Escócia, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1585, em Stirling, Stirlingshire, Scotland, com 52 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isobel McGowan родилась в 1533 г. в местности клаккманнан, клаккманнаньшир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Thomas McGowan было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Marion Cunningham, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Sharp в 1555 г. в местности клаккманнаньшир, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1585 г. в местности стирлинг, стирлингшир, Шотландия в возрасте 52 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isobel McGowan nació en 1533, en Clackmannan, Clackmannanshire, Scotland, su papá, Thomas McGowan, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Marion Cunningham, tenía 27 años. Se casó con John Sharp en 1555, en Clackmannanshire, Escocia, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1585, en Stirling, Stirlingshire, Scotland, a la edad de 52 años.
